---
title: "Wrap a stimulus"
subtitle: "`ImageStimulus`, `VideoStimulus`, `AudioStimulus`, `TextStimulus` - typed inputs with time."
sidebar: start
---

## What this page covers

Extraction starts from a stimulus object or from an input that can be coerced into one. The wrapper validates shape and timing before any feature code runs.

```{python}
import numpy as np

from natural_features.core.stimulus import (
    AudioStimulus,
    ImageStimulus,
    TextStimulus,
    VideoStimulus,
)

image = ImageStimulus.from_array(np.zeros((8, 8, 3)), onset_s=0.5, duration_s=1.0)
video = VideoStimulus.from_array(np.zeros((4, 8, 8, 3)), fps=2.0)
audio = AudioStimulus.from_array(np.zeros(1600), sr_hz=16000)
text = TextStimulus("The scene opens on a quiet room.")

{
    "image_shape": image.image.shape,
    "video_times": video.frame_times_s.tolist(),
    "audio_samples": audio.samples.shape[0],
    "text": text.text,
}
```

## Refuses to

- `ImageStimulus` rejects non-numeric arrays, empty dimensions, and arrays outside `(H, W)` or `(H, W, C)`.
- `VideoStimulus` rejects arrays that are not `(T, H, W)` or `(T, H, W, C)`.
- `AudioStimulus` rejects arrays that are not one- or two-dimensional.
- `TextStimulus` rejects non-string text.

## Read next

[List and plan features](02-list-and-plan-features.qmd)
